content.problemStatement:
For a set of positive integers SSS, let's define gcd(S)\mathrm{gcd}(S)gcd(S) as the greatest integer which divides each element of SSS. If gcd(S)=1\mathrm{gcd}(S) = 1gcd(S)=1, the set SSS is called coprime. For example, the set {7,12,15}\{7, 12, 15\}{7,12,15} is coprime, but {6,12,15}\{6, 12, 15\}{6,12,15} is not coprime, since every element of this set is divisible by 333.

Your task is to find an integer sequence A0,A1,…,AN−1A_0, A_1, \ldots, A_{N-1}A0​,A1​,…,AN−1​ such that:


for each valid iii, 1≤Ai≤1091 \le A_i \le 10^91≤Ai​≤109
A0,A1,…,AN−1A_0, A_1, \ldots, A_{N-1}A0​,A1​,…,AN−1​ are pairwise distinct
for each valid iii, the set {Ai,A(i+1)%N}\{A_i, A_{(i+1)\%N}\}{Ai​,A(i+1)%N​} is not coprime (%\%% denotes the modulo operator)
for each valid iii, the set {Ai,A(i+1)%N,A(i+2)%N}\{A_i, A_{(i+1)\%N}, A_{(i+2)\%N}\}{Ai​,A(i+1)%N​,A(i+2)%N​} is coprime
It is possible that there is no solution. If there are multiple solutions, you may find any one.
constraints: The sum of N over all test cases does not exceed 10^6
content.inputFormat: The first line of the input contains a single integer T denoting the number of test cases. The description of T test cases follows. The first and only line of each test case contains a single integer N.
content.outputFormat: For each test case: If there is no solution, print a single line containing the integer -1. Otherwise, print a single line containing N space-separated integers A0, A1, …, AN-1.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For N = 3, the sequence is 6, 10, 15. For N = 4, the sequence is 374, 595, 1365, 858.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use the concept of coprime numbers. We need to find a sequence of numbers such that each pair of consecutive numbers is not coprime, but each set of three consecutive numbers is coprime. We can start by choosing the first number, and then iteratively choose the next number such that it is not coprime with the previous number, but is coprime with the number before that.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N) — where T is the number of test cases and N is the maximum length of the sequence
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) — where N is the maximum length of the sequence
